user_report.issue: The Jyutping schema appears to use `p rather than the single grave accent ` as the Mandarin luna_pinyin reverse-lookup trigger.
user_report.requested_change: Use the single grave accent as the trigger for luna_pinyin reverse lookup.
user_report.observed_error: When typing in reverse lookup, the app is very slow and shows: 執行操作時發生錯誤。如輸入法不能正常運作，請重新載入頁面。 / An error occurred while performing the operation. If the input method does not work properly, please reload the page.
user_report.performance_note: The user suspects the slowness/error may share the same root cause as the M25 startup/typing performance issues.

local_verification.typeduck_v1_1_2_note: The captured TypeDuck v1.1.2 jyut6ping3 schema copy also uses prefix `p for luna_pinyin. M25-DOGFOOD-08 is therefore a new web dogfooding correction request, not a silent continuation of the M24 `p... assumption.
local_verification.current_bare_reverse_lookup_note: Follow-up review found the desktop reverse_lookup tag owns bare ` but has no translator/dictionary, so it is a vestigial empty slot rather than a functional Jyutping lookup.
local_verification.functional_secondary_lookup_note: The functional secondary lookup paths are currently luna_pinyin on `p, loengfan on `l, and cangjie on `c. Bare ` should become luna_pinyin; retained secondary lookups should move to explicit non-bare triggers, preferably a v-prefixed namespace such as `vl and `vc to avoid normal pinyin initial shadowing.
local_verification.user_clarification: The intended M25 behavior is that bare ` typed inside the jyut6ping3 schema triggers luna_pinyin reverse lookup. The web UI should explicitly show which trigger maps to which lookup/schema.

plan_change: Track as M25-DOGFOOD-08. The implementation should change the TypeDuck-Web Jyutping luna_pinyin reverse-lookup trigger to bare `, remove the vestigial bare-grave reverse_lookup tag unless a real consumer is proven, move retained secondary lookups to explicit triggers such as `vl and `vc, update visible per-schema trigger hints, capture browser evidence for `zhe and secondary triggers if retained, and keep the slow/error symptom linked to M25-DOGFOOD-01/M25-DOGFOOD-03 unless repro proves a distinct reverse-lookup failure.
